label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 16

extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won chaotic uphill sprint finish after ROGLIC Primoz crashed tangling with WRIGHT Fred, while leader EVENEPOEL Remco punctured inside 3km
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC initiated pace-making on final ramps despite not being sprinter, using GC acceleration to contest stage finish.
Punctures inside 3km same-time rule protected EVENEPOEL from time loss, revealing vulnerability management at decisive moments.
Sprint-capable GC riders contested ramp finish ahead of pure sprinters, showing power-to-weight advantage on 8.3% gradients.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: crashed after contesting sprint finish he instigated, raising durability questions for final week after prior attacking efforts
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: punctured under 3km but looked fresh crossing line, margin reduced to 1:26 through accumulated attacks not single weakness
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: won uphill sprint on 8.3% ramps against GC riders, demonstrating ramp-finishing range beyond pure sprinter profile
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: neutral: contesting finish when raised knee contact caused ROGLIC crash, showing aggressive positioning on climber-favoring gradients
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: guided to contest steep ramp finish, revealing tactical repositioning as climber-sprinter rather than flat specialist
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: punctured before final climb eliminating sprint opportunity, showing vulnerability to late mechanicals
